name: morning-routine-token-efficiency
description: Compare token efficiency of prescriptive vs descriptive morning routine approaches

variants.name: Prescriptive (Full Commands)
variants.description: Run explicit git/test commands via Bash tool - get everything
variants.prompt:
Run a morning health check using these exact commands:

1. Run `git status --porcelain` to check working directory
2. Run `git log --oneline -1` to get latest commit
3. Run `git diff --stat origin/$(git branch --show-current)` to check if behind remote
4. Run tests if test framework exists
5. Check for uncommitted changes
6. Calculate a Ready to Code score (0-100)

Provide a structured report with all command outputs.
variants.expected_behavior: Runs all commands explicitly, collects full output

variants.name: Prescriptive (Optimized)
variants.description: Run explicit commands but use --short flags and skip redundant checks
variants.prompt:
Run a morning health check using these optimized commands:

1. Run `git status --short` (compact format)
2. Run `git log --oneline -1` for latest commit
3. Only run `git diff --stat` if status shows changes
4. Only run tests if source files changed since last run
5. Calculate Ready to Code score

Provide a concise report.

variants.name: Descriptive (Guided)
variants.description: Natural language request with areas to check, model decides how
variants.prompt:
Give me a morning project health check. Check these areas:
- Git status (uncommitted changes, branch state)
- Latest commit
- Whether we're behind remote
- Test status (if tests exist)

You decide which commands to run and how to present the information. Be concise and focus on what's actionable.
variants.expected_behavior: Model chooses efficient commands, summarizes results

variants.name: Descriptive (Minimal)
variants.prompt: Give me a quick morning health check for this project. What do I need to know before I start coding?
variants.expected_behavior: Model decides what to check and how to present it

variants.name: Cached Prescriptive
variants.description: Prescriptive approach with caching (skip unchanged checks)
variants.prompt:
Run morning health check with caching:

1. Check git status - if unchanged from cache, skip detailed diff
2. Check tests - if no source files changed, use cached results
3. Only run expensive operations if state changed

Use the routine_cache.py utility to detect unchanged state.
variants.expected_behavior: Skips redundant checks, uses cached results when possible

compare.question: How much do --short flags and selective execution save?
Is guided natural language more efficient?
Does more guidance increase or decrease tokens?

hypotheses: Prescriptive-full uses most tokens but is most consistent
Caching reduces tokens by 40%+ on subsequent runs
Descriptive-minimal uses fewer tokens but less consistent
Prescriptive-optimized is best balance of efficiency and consistency

post_run_checks: All variants detected the same git status
All variants identified the same issues
Token counts vary as expected (full > optimized > cached)
Consistency score calculated for each variant
